title: Tried using an LLM to refine my trading strategy — it actually improved, but I’m not sure if it’s legit
author: Renov_J
selftext:
I’ve been experimenting with a small LLM-based feature that reads my backtest results and suggests how to improve the strategy.  
The idea is simple:  
run → analyze → AI gives feedback → apply → re-test.  
Sort of like having an assistant that looks at your stats and says “hey, maybe filter this condition”.

I tested it on BTC 1-min data (May → Nov 2025, 6 months).  
Started with a really basic RSI setup (TP 2 %, SL 1 %).  
Then I let the LLM iterate three times, each time suggesting tweaks based on performance.

The numbers surprised me a bit:

* Sharpe: 0.04 → 0.32
* Drawdown Ratio: 1.12 → 0.27
* Total Return: around 21 % (no fees or slippage included)

The AI added things like:

* skipping low-volatility periods,
* filtering out weak-trend shorts,
* combining multi-timeframe indicators.

Each change actually made the equity curve smoother — I attached screenshots for all 4 stages (Step 0 → Step 3).

Step0. Basic strategy using 1h RSI

* Total Return **8.5%**
* Drawdown ratio **1.12**
* Sharpe **0.042**

https://preview.redd.it/ko6cfkgkjtzf1.png?width=904&format=png&auto=webp&s=3def60230fe2de06621ed6406aef00af2f1062da



Step1. Enhanced Long Signal Precision by added Daily Stoch %K ≥ 70 to the Long Condition

* Total Return **15%** *(76% enhanced)*
* Drawdown ratio **0.72** *(-35% reduced)*
* Sharpe **0.09** *(114% enhanced)*

https://preview.redd.it/0k3s7ffkjtzf1.png?width=904&format=png&auto=webp&s=402f7e1060f3f1ad081caa5eaf43af95c7c26abd

Step2. Added Weak Trend Short Filter: Short only when 15m ADX ≤ 30. Improved trading quality by avoiding short positions outside of weak trend zones.

* Total Return **24%** *(60% enhanced)*
* Drawdown ratio **0.47** *(-35% reduced)*
* Sharpe **0.14** *(55% enhanced)*

https://preview.redd.it/a9nsu853mtzf1.png?width=904&format=png&auto=webp&s=aff85630b1a5281af3a17f5468e81425c3854788

Step3. Added Volatility Filter: Trade only when Daily BB Width % > 10 to avoid low volatility drawdown compression and stability increase.

* Total Return **21%** *(-13% decreased)*
* Drawdown ratio **0.27** *(43% reduced)*
* Sharpe **0.32** *(128% enhanced)*

https://preview.redd.it/2t4osefkjtzf1.png?width=904&format=png&auto=webp&s=dfc11edb048b21f2c2aab600c7046a6645c91ecc

It looks great, but honestly I’m not sure if this is a legit improvement or just overfitting that looks smart.  
The LLM explanations sound convincing, but I know how tricky hindsight can be.

Has anyone else tried using an LLM directly in their backtesting or strategy tuning?  
Would love to know if you found a reliable way to tell when it’s genuinely improving vs just curve-fitting.
